reviews.review_msg: There is only so much one can say about a professor like Shawn. His teaching style was odd and clunky as he spent a lot of time going over definitions for the most basic concepts. Exam questions make little sense and often are not properly proofread before going to print. Grading is incredibly unclear and harsh with no feedback.

Professor Tan is clearly passionate about what he teaches, however I think his delivery can be poor and it's hard to engage with his lectures. That being said, he makes everything you need to know very accessible. Attendance is not mandatory so easy to fit into your schedule. Just be prepared to take extra time to teach yourself a bit.

Took this class as an elective and it was a mistake. Only good thing is he posts the lecture recordings so you don't have to go to class. Instructions aren't always clear and the tests had grammar problems which affected the responses.. made it very confusing/stressful. Weekly quizzes and papers based on that week's chapter. Sooo much writing.

Super super super nice lady. Extremely incompetent prof. Memorize slides/summaries of readings and spew it out on exams/papers; you don't need to show understanding of any kind just write as much BS as you can and you get handed an A+! So weird but it works.
Tough homework assignments, would advise asking for help on these. Other than that attend all classes and you should get a great grade.
He is great nowadays. He is underrated here.

Lectures consistent of student presentations of an article every week and the rest of the class is him talking about a subject he wants to. Things were not well organized, and hard to study for. An 80 was considered a C and we did not know this at all. He is a very nice guy though and I liked him as a person. Though over all not a very hard class.

He's a good guy, unfortunately not in COGS himself so he can't speak to a lot of the CS or Psyc topics, engaging and teaches well though. Vague in marking as everyone else says but you can also just ask him questions over email or before/after class and he will answer. He's reasonable and if you have issues with group mates you can talk to him.
